title: Contain _prunelib.py monolith accretion — continue the _prunepolicy extraction
evidence: _prunelib.py is the repo's largest module at 1,365 LOC / 49 functions, co-locating transcript parsing, all phase strategies, backup/restore, metrics, and audit emission in one file. This branch already extracted the host-neutral planning core into _prunepolicy.py (SemanticEntry/PrunePolicy/plan_prune, consumed by _prunelib, prune-transcript.py, and ca-pi's pi-bridge.py) — the right direction — but the remaining engine keeps growing in place and sits above the oversized-file threshold the lens flags.
impact: Review and test surface for transcript-integrity code (a corruption-sensitive area by its own header guarantees) is concentrated in one oversized unit; new prune phases will accrete here by default.
recommendation: Follow the _prunepolicy precedent: peel the phase/strategy implementations and the backup/restore IO into sibling modules, keeping _prunelib as the orchestrating engine. Low urgency; opportunistic with the next prune feature.
acceptance_criteria: _prunelib.py reduced below ~900 LOC with strategies/IO in sibling modules
prune parity and integrity tests pass unchanged
